data.title: Full Sail University Named a 2026 Top Undergrad and Grad School for Game Design by The Princeton Review
data.description: In Winter Park, Florida, on March 25, 2026, Full Sail University's game design studies program has been recognized as one of the top 50 undergraduate programs and top 25 graduate programs in the country by The Princeton Review®. This accolade highlights the university's excellence in game design education, affirming its position among the nation's leading institutions in the field.

data.title: Piramal Pharma Solutions Commemorates 20th Anniversary at Morpeth Facility with Procurement of Korsch X3 Tablet Press
data.description: A cutting-edge tablet press known as the Korsch X3 has been specifically designed to meet the requirements of the facility. Featuring complete Band 5 containment, this press allows for the secure production of potent medications on both clinical and commercial levels. This advancement positions Morpeth to effectively cater to the increasing demand for such products.

data.title: Erick Hernández Gallego Appointed President of ICC, Mexico Chapter Environment Commission
data.description: On March 25, 2026, Erick Hernández Gallego, a Shareholder at Greenberg Traurig, was assigned as the president of the International Chamber of Commerce. Hernández Gallego leads the Environmental Practice and co-chairs the Energy & Natural Resources Practice at the Mexico City office.

data.title: Axiad Named 2026 Cyber Top Companies Award Winner by Enterprise Security Tech
data.description: A tech company based in Santa Clara, California, revealed on March 25, 2026, that it has been recognized as one of the four champions in the 2026 awards. Axiad, known for its expertise in identity visibility, intelligence, and credential management, aims to merge human and non-human identities through a single platform tailored for contemporary businesses.

data.title: Microsoft, Nvidia Just Fused AI And Atoms — 8 Nuclear Stocks In Focus
data.description: Microsoft and Nvidia, both listed on NASDAQ as MSFT and NVDA, have joined forces to introduce artificial intelligence into the nuclear energy sector. This recent collaboration aims to revolutionize the design, approval, construction, and operation of reactors, which could potentially lead to a significant increase in nuclear-focused stock prices. Through combining Microsoft's cloud and AI infrastructure with Nvidia's cutting-edge computing technologies, the partnership is set to simplify processes, minimize manual labor, and enhance workflow consistency in nuclear projects. This initiative involves replacing conventional, paper-intensive methods with a unified digital environment that enhances the efficiency, predictability, and scalability of nuclear plant projects. By utilizing AI and high-fidelity digital simulations, this platform accelerates design iterations, streamlines regulatory evaluations, and... More information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Karman Stock Tests Entry Amid 43% Rally. Earnings Are Due.
data.description: Karman Holdings, a company popular for its IPO defense strategy, recently disclosed its Q4 results on Wednesday. The stock has been experiencing a significant boost, attributed to the current surge in defense spending. The company's shares have seen a remarkable 43% increase, propelling the stock to test an entry point. Investors are eagerly anticipating the upcoming earnings report.

data.title: Denali Jumps, Pulling Regenxbio Higher, On Early FDA Approval
data.description: Denali Therapeutics achieved FDA approval on Wednesday for the first new drug in two decades to address a rare genetic disorder. This groundbreaking development led to a surge in Denali's stock, consequently boosting Regenxbio's shares as well.

data.title: Power Integrations Stock Sees RS Rating Jump to 83
data.description: Power Integrations has experienced an upgrade in Relative Strength Rating to 83, indicating a positive trend in technical performance. This development showcases an improvement in the company's standing and offers insight into its potential growth and investment opportunities.

data.title: TSS Stock Earns 85 Relative Strength Rating
data.description: TSS has recently received an upgrade in Relative Strength Rating, indicating a positive trend in its technical performance. The stock now holds an impressive 85 Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade highlights the company's improving strength compared to its peers in the market.

data.title: CrowdStrike and AWS Announce Jazz as the Winner of the 2026 Cybersecurity Startup Accelerator, Supported by NVIDIA Inception
data.description: In Austin, Texas & San Francisco today, CrowdStrike revealed Jazz as the champion of the third yearly Cybersecurity Startup Accelerator, held in conjunction with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and NVIDIA under the NVIDIA Inception program during RSA 2026. Jazz's pioneering work in AI-powered data loss prevention (DLP) stood out, showcasing its ability to tackle essential security obstacles encountered by contemporary businesses in the AI age. The accelerator for 2026 saw an impressive turnout, with close to 1,000 startups participating.

data.title: Israel Corp. Reports Results for Fourth Quarter and Annual 2025
data.description: Tel Aviv, Israel, March 25, 2026 - Israel Corporation Ltd. (ILCO) shared its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025. In Q4 2025, ILCO reported a $32 million share in ICL profit, a decrease from $31 million in Q4 2024. The annual figures for 2025 indicated a share of $99 million compared to $179 million in 2024. Additionally, ILCO reported a $1 million loss in Prodalim net profit, while in 2024 it showed a $2 million profit. There was a $2 million amortization of excess cost in held companies in Q4 2025, a decrease from $1 million in Q4 2024. Furthermore, financing, G&A, and other expenses at ILCO headquarters amounted to $3 million in Q4 2025, an increase from $2 million in Q4 2024. Full details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Blink Charging Q4 Preview: Crypto Payments, New Chargers, Improved Costs — Will Shares Hit $1?
data.description: Blink Charging Co (NASDAQ: BLNK) is expected to release its fourth-quarter financial results after the market closes. Analysts are estimating fourth-quarter revenue to be around $28.56 million, a decrease from $30.18 million in the same period last year. The company has a history of missing revenue estimates, having done so in four out of the last five quarters. The anticipated loss per share for the fourth quarter is 11 cents, an improvement from a loss of 15 cents in the previous year. Blink Charging has surpassed earnings per share estimates in six out of the last 10 quarters, as demonstrated in the third quarter. Investors will be keeping a close eye on these key metrics to gauge the company's performance.

data.title: Could XRP Dip Towards $1 Before An 'Explosive' Breakout?
data.description: Analysts are observing XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) experiencing range-bound price movements, prompting speculation of a potential pullback before an anticipated breakout. In a recent podcast, Cryptoinsightuk suggested that XRP might dip towards $1 before a significant upward movement, as traders navigate short-term uncertainties amidst a positive long-term outlook. The cryptocurrency is currently consolidating, with its price confined within a wedge formation, indicating the possibility of either a breakout towards higher levels or a sustained downward trend in the near future. There are projections of a potential decline towards $1 by June.
